name: Small Varnian Banner Pike
description: A small banner pike bearing Varnia's symbol. It looks like it could be used as a weapon in a pinch.

name: Small Vellua Pirates Banner Pike
description: A small banner pike bearing the Vellua Pirates' symbol. It looks like it could be used as a weapon in a pinch.

name: Small Wells Banner Pike
description: A small banner pike bearing House Wells' symbol. It looks like it could be used as a weapon in a pinch.

name: Small Wells Brigade Banner Pike
description: A small banner pike bearing House Wells' symbol. It looks like it could be used as a weapon in a pinch.

name: Small Wyvernflames Banner Pike
description: A small banner pike bearing the Wyvernflames' symbol. It looks like it could be used as a weapon in a pinch.

name: Soporific Blowpipe
description: A blowpipe made by Pororin Forest Guardians to fire poison darts. Using mushrooms and herbs gathered in the forest, a sleeping potion is brewed. This dart its powerful enough to send even a troll to dreamland in an instant. Waking up after a hit feels oddly refreshing.

name: Sorcerer's Massage Stick
description: A weapon made by chance. A sorcerer, who frequently snapped massage sticks due to their overwhelming arm strength, used magic to enhance one. The result was an object far more sinister than its original purpose intended.

name: Sorcerer's Staff
description: A staff used by Tariv's sorcerers. Those who can't wield magic sometimes mistake it for a spear. Tariv developed spells to stand against Hexe Marie's darkness, studying magic meant to protect, guard, and preserve.

name: Soul Spear
description: A spear forged from souls harvested by the followers of Antumbra. Delivering a finishing blow releases the trapped spirits within, eroding the sanity of nearby enemies. Requires a brief cooldown after the attack.

name: Southern Wandering Troll's Crossbow
description: A crossbow favored by wandering trolls of the southern region. Its sturdiness is recognized as that of a reliable weapon, even across varied environments.

name: Spada Sword
description: A sword that slew the Flame Dragon. Originally an honorable heirloom passed down through generations of Urdavah's Dragon Knights, it was turned to serve a new master after Tristan betrayed his homeland.

name: Spear of Righteousness
description: A spear issued to the Righteous Inquisitors. Their "righteousness" is a fanatical creed known only to themselves, not a virtue all can share.

name: Spear of the Fleeting
description: A spear wielded by Hexe Sanctuary's earthen warriors. This spear serves in rituals that create new victims for Hexe Marie, birthing more tormented brothers shrouded in darkness.

name: Specter Sword
description: A sword for the Specter Knight summon.

name: Spencer Pistol
description: A pistol crafted by House Spencer. While production was discontinued when the house fell, a few are said to still remain in private collections. It is one of the few remaining pieces of evidence showing that House Spencer once existed in the world.

name: Spine of the Earth
description: A sturdy mace crafted from interwoven stones. Its origins are shrouded in mystery, and its braided cords and feather adornments suggest value beyond simple weaponry.

name: Split Tooth
description: A greataxe once wielded by the White Bear of the High Mountain. Its unusual twin-bladed head splits into two prongs, making the enemy feel as if they are being snapped at by the fangs of a massive beast.

name: Staff of the Fleeting
description: A staff wielded by Hexe Sanctuary's earthen warriors. Shattering their jars will spill clay, brimstone, and bones, which are all vital for Hexe Marie's profane creations.

name: Steel Blaster
description: A standard-issue blaster made with steel. It was developed after the need for a highly mobile heavy firearm became clear in war, but it's said to be too heavy and unwieldy for just anyone to use.

name: Survivor's Solitude
description: The sword held by the last king of a forgotten kingdom until his final moments. It is said to be engraved with a pattern symbolizing the ancient royal house, but no one remains to recognize it as such.

name: Sword of Greed
description: The Knight of Fortune's sword. He clad himself head to toe in gleaming gold and adorned it with jewels. With this sword, he took countless lives, yet he could never escape his thirst for greed to the very end.

name: Sword of the Fleeting
description: A sword wielded by the earthen swordsmen of Hexe Sanctuary. Hexe Marie calls her earthen soldiers her children, but she discards them easily, raising doubts about what "child" means to her.

name: Sword of the Lord
description: An antique weapon crafted through a sophisticated process. Originally forged for the Celeste royal family, its whereabouts became a mystery after Hernand lost its status as a kingdom. It is said that Hornsplitter spent an unimaginable fortune to attain this blade.

name: Sword of the Wolf
description: A well-worn sword, marked by countless battles. Though a lone wolf seems solitary, it longs for the pack that completes it. Scattered, perhaps, but a pack never truly disappears.

name: Sword of Wayward Woods
description: A sword wrapped with poisonous vines, commonly used by Mistwood Hunters. It's not very powerful as a blade, but inflicts anyone cut by it with fatal poison.

name: Sydmon Bow
description: A bow bearing the name of the ordinary blacksmith, Sydmon. "Sydmon drew his last breath while sharpening the blade of the axe, having spent the last of his strength."

name: Sydmon Dagger
description: A dagge bearing the name of the ordinary blacksmith, Sydmon. "Sydmon's craft never rose above a wretched sort of mediocrity."

name: Sydmon Hammer
description: A hammer bearing the name of the ordinary blacksmith, Sydmon. "When given but a single chance, he steeled himself to break the curse of mediocrity."

name: Sydmon Longsword
description: A longsword bearing the name of Sydmon, the ordinary blacksmith. "Day and night, with all his passion and soul, Sydmon burned himself out forging that axe."

name: Sydmon Spear
description: A spear bearing the name of the ordinary blacksmith, Sydmon. "At last, Sydmon forged it so fine that any soul would marvel at it, but..."

name: Sydmon Sword
description: A sword bearing the name of the ordinary blacksmith, Sydmon. "There's a tale every soul who works iron knows well. It's the story of Sydmon, the ordinary blacksmith."

name: Tardik Axe
description: An axe of the now vanished Tardik Freeswords. "A mere clerical error can sometimes forge a hero's name. Such is the tale of the Tardic Freewords."

name: Tardik Bow
description: A bow of the now vanished Tardik Freeswords. "Tardik's name was mistakenly passed down as a lone hero's, but it was built by the blood and sweat of ordinary freeswords."

name: Tardik Greataxe
description: A greataxe of the now vanished Tardik Freeswords. "When the army's commander arrived and asked to the last dying warrior who he was, he answered that he was Tardik."

name: Tardik Longsword
description: A longsword of the now vanished Tardik Freeswords. "In their final battle, the Tardik Freeswords gave their all to defend an innocent town."

name: Tardik Warhammer
description: A warhammer of the now vanished Tardik Freeswords. "The commander, having misunderstood the warrior's last words, etched the name 'Tardik' into the records as the name of a man."

name: Tauria Curved Sword
description: A curved sword used by Crowcaller. The golden-hued curved blade slices through the wind, leaving blood and death in its wake. And it is said that those who fall to this sword become sacrificial offerings to the crows. The moment it is swung, the cry of a crow echoes across the sky.

name: Thalwynd Hammer
description: A hammer crafted in Calphade. They say there are no ordinary civilians in Thalwynd. That may be an exaggeration, but it's rare to find a Thalwynd local who can't wield a hammer.

name: Thalwynd Longsword
description: A longsword forged in Calphade. Even those no longer serving in the army often keep a fine blade at home. Such is the custom in Thalwynd.

name: Thalwynd Warhammer
description: A warhammer crafted in Calphade. It reflects Thalwynd's military pride and keen eye for weapons. The townspeople would never allow their name to be on anything less than a first-class weapon.

name: The Grove's Thorn
description: A weapon crafted from thorns naturally shed by the Sacred Tree of Roothold. It is said that branches forcibly broken from the tree lack the strength required to make a weapon. Consequently, it is a custom in Roothold to offer sincere rituals to the tree and collect only the branches that have fallen.

name: Thorn of Dark Pursuit
description: A massive spear once wielded by Antumbra's Spear, one of Antumbra's three great deacons. A thorny ring stands out just beneath the blade. With this spear, Antumbra's Spear pierced souls to offer them to the darkness.

name: Timberham Axe
description: An axe crafted for the Timberham Sawmill. Surprisingly well-balanced, it serves as a tool and weapon. It's a reliable companion for any traveler seeking practical arms.

name: Tiny Greymane Banner Pike
description: A tiny banner pike bearing the Greymane's symbol. It looks like it could be used as a weapon in a pinch.

name: Tommaso Guard's Dagger-Tipped Spear
description: A spear used by the Tommaso Guard. Three daggers have been bound together to serve as the spearhead. It's tied tightly enough, and seems fit for the guard's use.

name: Tommasoan Bow
description: A bow crafted by the Tommaso guards. It is unwise to assume the guards will protect you. In Tommaso, everyone lives by the law of strength.

name: Tommasoan Contribution Banner Pike
description: A banner pike engraved with the pattern representing Tommaso. It is lashed to a long shaft, allowing it to be used as a weapon. Only those who have contributed to Tashkalp are permitted to possess it.

name: Tormented Soul Bow
description: A horn bow once wielded by a warrior from a distant land, where dawn spreads in radiant splendor. Leaving behind a homeland of lush bamboo groves, the warrior is said to have roamed the world in search of wondrous adventures.

name: Traitor's Spear
description: A spear once favored by Cassius Morten, known as the Silver Armor, before his betrayal. After betraying Calphade, he refused to use it, insisting on blunt weapons as if to prove he was better than Stefan Lanford.

name: Troll Hook Greataxe
description: A greataxe forged not for men, but for trolls. Though its hooked blade was meant for nonlethal restraint, the weapon's sheer destructive power overshadows the intended purpose, and it's rarely in such way.

name: Trumpet Shotgun
description: A shotgun as menacing as its massive barrel. The wisest way to deal with desert raiders is to avoid bloodshed altogether. A large, intimidating gun offers silent persuasion, encouraging enemies to look for easier prey.

name: Twilight Messengers' Bow
description: A bow crafted by the Twilight Messengers. They shoot first, then demand your valuables. There's no crime they wouldn't commit for wealth.

name: Twisted Verdict
description: A longsword once wielded by Corrupted Caliburn. It passes judgment on those who stand against its aim to shroud the world in equal darkness.

name: Valgash's Incomplete Work
description: An unfinished invention by Valgash, Gorthak's inventor. Is it part of a larger device? The hand-shaped ornament on the side surely serves some clever purpose.

name: Varnian Contribution Banner Pike
description: A banner pike engraved with the emblem of Varnia. The long pole features a unique Varnian decorative design, allowing it to be used as a weapon. Only those who have contributed to Tashkalp are permitted to possess it.

name: Varnian Dagger
description: A dagger made in the Varnian style. Blocked by the Crimson Desert, Varnia is an unknown realm to most Pywel people. Rare and exotic goods brought out through trade spark people's curiosity.

name: Varnian Sword
description: A sword forged by the craftsmen of Varnia. In Varnia, a fine blade is a symbol of wealth, honor, and duty to defend the holy city.

name: Verak Greatsword
description: A folding greatsword made by House Spencer. Made with experimental techniques, it is not easy to handle—likely usable properly only by members of the house. Built with a special mechanism, the blade can be folded for carrying when not in use.

name: Vessel of Dark Pursuit
description: A longsword once wielded by Antumbra's Sword, one of the three guides of Antumbra. Legend has it that he gave himself as an offering by impalement with his very blade, and in exchange, was reborn as a vessel to contain the darkness of Umbra. Wielding his bestowed power, he hungers to harbor even more darkness.

name: Vilancia Pistol
description: A pistol once popular among the Demenissian nobility. It is said to be a firearm frequently sought by the wealthy when pistol duels were at the height of their popularity. While its performance as a weapon was not bad, it was favored more because it did not easily malfunction even when handled with unskilled hands.

name: Volcanic Blaster
description: A massive blaster once wielded by Ravok.  It was the weapon of choice for the leader of the Savage Fangs, a nomadic bandit clan from the Crimson Desert. It is crude to the point of recklessness, yet boasts immense power.

name: Volono Rapier
description: One of the pair of twin blades crafted as a token of love. Among lovers in Pywel, it's popular to commission a matching set of weapons and share them between each other. This lone rapier hints at the tragic fate of its owners.

name: Volono Sword
description: One of the pair of twin blades crafted as a token of love. Among the lovers of Pywel, it is a popular custom to share a matching pair of weapons. This lone sword hints at the tragic end of its owners.

name: Vow of the Dead King
description: A halberd once wielded by Beloth, the Darksworn. Bloodstain remains from the pitch-black day. It is said that Beloth could not let go of this spear even as he lost his throne in the south, and froze to death in the northern snowfields.

name: Warspike Bow
description: A bow crafted in Fort Warspike. This is the type of bow Walter Lanford favored before becoming the notorious leader of the Bleed Bandits. If he had succeeded in releasing the arrow aimed at his stepfather, the history of Pywel would have changed.

name: Warspike Spear
description: A spear forged at the Warspike Spearmaker. The House Alfonso combat manuals, passed down through generations, cover not only individual martial arts but also large-scale tactics for commanding units of spearmen. To support these strategies, the House ensures their standard-issue spears balance high performance with mass-production efficiency.

name: Wells Mace
description: A mace crafted at Thornbriar Fortress in Wells. True to its name, the design is modeled after a blooming flower bud. Like a flower forged from steel, it symbolizes that House Wells' loyalty to the Thorel Dynasty will never wither.

name: Wells Musket
description: A musket crafted only in Wells. As Grand General Bastier's power rose, Duke Wells wisely built an independent force, free from the capital's control. In hindsight, it was a superb decision.

name: Wells Sword
description: A standard-issue weapon once used by the Wells military. It became so popular after passing House Wells' rigorous standards that soldiers from other units and adventurers would pay out of pocket to acquire it. However, with the introduction of a new standard-issue sword, it has since been labeled an outdated model.

name: Wheel of the Last Toll
description: A massive greataxe once wielded by Bradie Gu. An iron wheel is mounted in place of a blade. The headgear hung as ornaments belonged to those Bradie Gu had killed. When charging forward with the wheel on the ground, it spins, reaping all life in its path.

name: White Wind Rapier
description: A rapier once used by Demeniss's guard captain. Its rapier-style wire guard wraps around the hand, and it was called the White Wind, the captain's nickname.

name: White Wood Bow
description: A bow crafted in the Pailune style. It feels more like an improvised tool made from nearby materials than a weapon built for long-term use. The Northerners have long been masters of bow crafting.

name: Wolf's Fang
description: Jian's sword. Known as a sword that symbolizes justice and determination. According to legends, this sword was made by Pailunese craftsmen using minerals that fell from the sky.

name: Wyvern Blaster
description: A blaster once wielded by Balthazar, the Wyvernflame. From the back of his dragon, he unleashed ruthless barrages of fire, ensuring Fort Windridge remained an impregnable stronghold.

name: Zargan Warhammer
description: A warhammer crafted by the orcs of the ironworks. The orcs are masters of their craft. Though made for work, the remarkable quality makes them just as fit for combat.
